title: Open Field
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bdpqk","text":"This protocol describes the assessment of spontaneous locomotor activity in mice using the open field test. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"995v0","text":"Locomotor behavior is recorded in an infrared actimeter chamber and analyzed using automated tracking software.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"94rbh","text":"See the General considerations and habituation protocol. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":8,"length":47}],"data":{}},{"key":"9aop6","text":"If experiment is done during the dark cycle, it is preferable to start the behaviour work at least one hour after the light-dark cycle change.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"8D4340A15E2D11F1ACB00A58A9FEAC02","url":"http://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.kxygxj834l8j/v1"}}}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"9ajrd","text":"When the recording starts, put a black paper on the laptop screen. ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bs691","text":"Experimenter should leave the room during the recording. If the experimenter wants to remain inside the room during the recording, he/she/them should stay away from the recording chambers and minimize any noise. It is advisable not to use a laptop computer, tablet, or cellphone during the recording time. The reason is that the these devises emit light that is visible to animals and noise, something that can perturb them. ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5v778","text":"A chamber partition can be used to test multiple mice at the same time.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"c43up","text":"Apparatus: SuperFlex Open Field (Omnitech, Superflex sensor version 4.6)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4mh5f","text":"Software: Fusion v5.6 SuperFlex (RRID:SCR_017972)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Mice are acclimated in the room where the open field test is performed 01:00:00 before the experiment
The locomotor behavior of mice is recorded using an infrared actimeter (Superflex sensor version 4.6, Omnitech) using the Fusion software (v5.6 Superflex Edition, RRID:SCR_017972).
Once testing is completed, the mouse identification tag number is noted, and mice are placed back into their home cage.
The number of feces pellets present in each open field box is also noted.
Mice spend a total of 00:30:00 in the chamber.
Subjects are placed in the chamber without acclimation and the recording is started.
The open field boxes are cleaned before testing the next mouse.
creator: Amandine Even
